question.label: Mentoring useful
question.name: What makes mentoring feel useful for you right now?
question.description: Drills into why mentoring still feels worthwhile and what need it is meeting.

answer: I am not sure the work I am doing is the work that gets recognized.

assessment.note: He named a recognition gap — uncertainty that his current work is the work that gets recognized — which is a strong growth deficit signal.

new_queue.label: Thread follow
new_queue.name: sure work doing — can you say more about what that means for you right now?

new_queue.label: Next-step picture
new_queue.name: What concrete path forward feels real to you right now?

new_queue.label: Support needed
new_queue.name: What would help make the next couple of weeks steadier for you?

history.answer_excerpt: I have been less present in rituals, yes.
I have been less present in rituals, yes.
I stayed focused on delivery because that felt controllable.
I stayed focused on delivery because that felt controllable.
Mentoring has been one place where I still feel useful.
I am not sure the work I am doing is the work that gets recognized.
